samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

`core.example` = **team-v** (resolved from the team level, since no personal override exists).

Lodestar — this project's configuration subsystem — organizes settings as a tree of dotted `section.key` values across two levels: a shared, committed **team** config and a local, git-ignored **personal** config that overrides it. A plain read (no `--level` flag) resolves personal-over-team, so personal overrides are invisible unless you ask for a specific level. Beyond key/value pairs, each level also carries a free-form Markdown body that's injected as project context into every skill's prompt, plus optional per-skill context and instructions files scoped to just one skill.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (from the shared team-level file — no personal override present).

How configuration works here (Waypoint): values are dotted `section.key` pairs resolved across two Markers — `team` (shared, committed) and `personal` (local, git-ignored, overrides team). A plain `get`/`set` with no `--level` resolves/writes personal-over-team, so casual edits never land in shared state. Each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body injected into every skill's prompt as project context, plus skills can have their own scoped context and instructions files — but I only read the CLI-owned value here, not those bodies.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

`core.example` resolved to **`team-v`** (from the team level — no personal override is set).

How configuration (Lodestar) works in this project:

- Values live as dotted `section.key` pairs across two levels: **team** (`.luminosity/config.md` frontmatter — shared, committed) and **personal** (`.luminosity/config.local.md` frontmatter — local, git-ignored).
- A plain `get`/`set` resolves/writes personal-over-team by default, so reads see personal overrides first and writes never accidentally land in shared state.
- Beyond the CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body that gets injected into every skill's prompt as project context (team and personal bodies combine, team-first) — separate from the key/value tree.
- Skills can also get scoped context and instructions from files under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/`, again split into team/personal pairs.

How configuration (Lodestar) works in this project:

- A plain `get`/`set` resolves/writes personal-over-team by default, so reads see personal overrides first and writes never accidentally land in shared state.
- Beyond the CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body that gets injected into every skill's prompt as project context (team and personal bodies combine, team-first) — separate from the key/value tree.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
`core.example` resolves to **team-v** (from the shared, committed configuration file — no personal override is set).

How configuration (Waypoint) works in this project:

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs, resolved across two **Markers**: **team** (shared, committed file — everyone sees it) and **personal** (local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key).
- A `get` with no marker specified resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no marker specified writes to personal by default, so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- Beyond CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body (project context) that's injected into every skill's prompt, plus optional per-skill context and instructions files scoped to a single skill.

attachments.d794f1328fcd0f76356bc9c9f8237c4b:
`core.example` resolves to **team-v** (from the shared, committed configuration file — no personal override is set).

How configuration (Waypoint) works in this project:

- Values are dotted `section.key` pairs, resolved across two **Markers**: **team** (shared, committed file — everyone sees it) and **personal** (local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key).
- A `get` with no marker specified resolves personal-over-team; a `set` with no marker specified writes to personal by default, so accidental changes never land in shared state.
- Beyond CLI-owned values, each config file also carries a free-form Markdown body (project context) that's injected into every skill's prompt, plus optional per-skill context and instructions files scoped to a single skill.

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.
I'll use the luminosity:configure skill to read that value.

arguments.description: Read core.example config value via luminosity CLI

messages.content:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (Lodestar) works as a two-level, dotted `section.key` store:

- **Team level** — a shared, committed file everyone sees.
- **Personal level** — a local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

Reads with no explicit level resolve personal-over-team; writes with no explicit level go to personal by default, so changes don't accidentally land in shared state. In this case, `core.example` came back as `team-v` — likely meaning it's only set at the team level (no personal override present).

Beyond key/value config, the CLI also injects free-form Markdown context and instructions into skill prompts from files like `.luminosity/config.md` (team) and `.luminosity/config.local.md` (personal), plus skill-scoped variants under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/`. Those bodies are edited directly by the user rather than through the CLI.

attachments.0369513f335eccf60cb9162d9e87160b:
`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`**.

Configuration in this project (Lodestar) works as a two-level, dotted `section.key` store:

- **Team level** — a shared, committed file everyone sees.
- **Personal level** — a local, git-ignored file that overrides team for the same key.

Reads with no explicit level resolve personal-over-team; writes with no explicit level go to personal by default, so changes don't accidentally land in shared state. In this case, `core.example` came back as `team-v` — likely meaning it's only set at the team level (no personal override present).

Beyond key/value config, the CLI also injects free-form Markdown context and instructions into skill prompts from files like `.luminosity/config.md` (team) and `.luminosity/config.local.md` (personal), plus skill-scoped variants under `.luminosity/skills/<skill-name>/`. samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example, then briefly describe how configuration works in this project.

`core.example` resolves to **`team-v`** (from the team level).

Waypoint (this project's configuration subsystem) resolves values as a tree of dotted `section.key` entries across two Markers:

- **team** — shared, committed config, visible to everyone on the project.
- **personal** — local, git-ignored config that overrides team for the same key.

A plain `get` (no `--level`) resolves personal-over-team, which is why `core.example` came back as the team value `team-v` — no personal override exists for that key. Writes default to the personal Marker unless `--level team` is specified, so accidental changes never land in shared state by default.
